title: Work, Energy and Power
learningObjectives: Calculate work done using W = Fd cosθ
Use conservation of energy to solve problems
Calculate power as rate of energy transfer
introText: Energy is the capacity to do work. Understanding how energy is transferred, transformed, and conserved is fundamental to mechanics.

steps.heading: Work Done
steps.body:
Work is done when a force moves an object through a displacement:

W = F d cosθ

where F is the force, d is the displacement, and θ is the angle between the force and displacement. Work is measured in Joules (J).

If the force is perpendicular to displacement (θ = 90°), no work is done.
steps.deepDive: Work is a scalar quantity (no direction). Negative work means the force opposes the motion, e.g. friction does negative work on a sliding object.

steps.heading: Kinetic and Potential Energy
steps.body:
Kinetic energy (KE) is the energy of motion:
KE = ½mv²

Gravitational potential energy (GPE) is stored due to height:
GPE = mgh

Elastic potential energy is stored in stretched/compressed springs:
EPE = ½kx²
steps.deepDive: The work-energy theorem states: W_net = ΔKE. The net work done on an object equals its change in kinetic energy.

steps.heading: Conservation of Energy
steps.body:
In an isolated system, total mechanical energy is conserved:

KE₁ + GPE₁ = KE₂ + GPE₂

When friction is present, energy is converted to thermal energy:

KE₁ + GPE₁ = KE₂ + GPE₂ + W_friction

Power is the rate of energy transfer: P = W/t = Fv (measured in Watts).
steps.deepDive: Conservation of energy is one of the most fundamental principles in physics. It applies across all energy forms – mechanical, electrical, thermal, chemical, nuclear.

steps.questionText: A 2 kg ball is dropped from a height of 5 m. What is its kinetic energy just before it hits the ground? (g = 10 m/s²)
steps.explanation: GPE at top = mgh = 2 × 10 × 5 = 100 J. By conservation of energy, KE just before impact = 100 J.

questions.questionText: A 500 W motor lifts a 10 kg box at constant speed. How high can it raise it in 4 seconds? (g = 10 m/s²)
